name: S'gaw Karen

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of S'gaw Karen (sgaw1245).

notes: Skeleton (lexicon-dependent). S'gaw Karen is a Karenic (Sino-Tibetan) language of Myanmar written in a Myanmar (Burmese-derived) abugida with Karen-specific letters and tone diacritics. Lexical tone, where present, is not orthographically recoverable. More fundamentally, grapheme-to-IPA for this script requires a per-word lexicon/pronunciation stratum that the engine does not bundle: syllable-final and stacked/sign-conditioned readings are not derivable letter-by-letter, so engine coverage is lexicon-dependent and effectively zero without that stratum. No grapheme map is asserted for this reason.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: S'gaw Karen (sgaw1245)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
